title: Wearable patch for reducing snoring activity
abstract: A patch can include a wearable component configured to allow the patch to be worn on a user, and a sensor component implemented relative to the wearable component. The sensor component can include a transducer configured to generate an electrical signal based on a snoring sound. In some embodiments, such a patch can be utilized in a system for reducing snoring of the user. The system can include a patch generating a first signal indicative of a snoring sound, and a control unit configured to generate a second signal based on the first signal, with the second signal being configured to allow actuation of a stimulus that reduces snoring activity associated with the snoring sound. The system can further include a sleep adjustment device in communication with the control unit and configured to generate the stimulus based on the second signal.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A system for reducing snoring activity, the system comprising: a patch configured to be secured to a skin of a user and generate a first signal indicative of a snoring sound; and a control unit configured to generate a second signal based on the first signal, the second signal configured to allow actuation of a stimulus that reduces snoring activity associated with the snoring sound. 2. The system of claim 1 wherein the user having the patch is a sleeping person generating the snoring sound. 3. The system of claim 1 wherein the control unit is implemented as part of the wearable patch. 4. The system of claim 1 wherein the control unit includes a device separated from the patch. 5. The system of claim 4 wherein the device is a wireless device, and the first signal includes a first wireless signal transmitted from the patch to the wireless device. 6. The system of claim 5 wherein the wireless device is a smartphone. 7. The system of claim 1 further comprising a sleep adjustment device in communication with the control unit and configured to generate the stimulus based on the second signal. 8. The system of claim 7 wherein the sleep adjustment device is part of an adjustable bed. 9. The system of claim 8 wherein the stimulus generated by the sleep adjustment device results in a change in an angle of a mattress of the adjustable bed. 10. The system of claim 7 wherein the sleep adjustment device is part of a mattress. 11. The system of claim 10 wherein the mattress is configured to be supported by a fixed frame. 12. The system of claim 10 wherein the sleep adjustment device is implemented as an integral part of the mattress, or as a pad configured to be placed on the mattress. 13. The system of claim 7 wherein the stimulus generated by the sleep adjustment device is a mechanical stimulus. 14. The system of claim 13 wherein the sleep adjustment device includes a plurality of actuator elements implemented such that one or more selected ones of the actuator elements are activated based on the second signal. 15. The system of claim 14 wherein each of the actuator elements is configured to provide a massaging vibration or a poking action as the stimulus when selected. 16. The system of claim 14 wherein the selected one or more ones of the actuator elements includes all of the actuator elements. 17. The system of claim 14 wherein the sleep adjustment device is further configured to sense sleeping position of the user, and the one or more actuator elements are selected based at least in part on the sensed sleeping position of the user. 18. The system of claim 17 wherein the one or more actuator elements are selected to induce a change in sleeping position of the user from the sensed sleeping position to a new sleeping position having a lower likelihood of snoring. 19. The system of claim 18 wherein the new position having the lower likelihood of snoring is determined based on a profile data associated with the user, the profile data included in or accessible by the control unit. 20. The system of claim 19 wherein the control unit includes a processor configured to build the profile data and/or to utilize the profile data. 21. The system of claim 20 wherein the processor is further configured to execute an algorithm including an artificial intelligence algorithm. 22. The system of claim 17 wherein the sleep adjustment device includes a plurality of pressure sensors. 23. The system of claim 22 wherein the plurality of pressure sensors are co located with respective ones of the plurality of actuator elements. 24. A system for reducing snoring activity, the system comprising: non-transitory computer readable medium including data representative of a sleep profile of a person, the sleep profile obtained from use of one or more patches each configured to be secured to a skin of the person and sense snoring of the person, such that the sleep profile includes information about a snoring event during which the person is likely to snore; a control unit in communication with the non-transitory computer readable medium and configured to generate a sleep plan for the person based at least in part on the information about the snoring event, the sleep plan including a control signal; and a sleep adjustment device configured to receive the control signal and provide a stimulus to the person, the stimulus selected to reduce the likelihood of snoring during a period associated with the snoring event. 25. The system of claim 24 wherein the control unit is configured to generate the sleep plan without further use of a patch by the person. 26. The system of claim 24 wherein the control unit includes a processor configured to generate the sleep plan utilizing an artificial intelligence algorithm. 27. The system of claim 24 wherein the control unit is configured to generate the sleep profile. 28. The system of claim 27 wherein the sleep profile is generated during a number of sleep periods. 29. The system of claim 28 wherein the sleep periods includes at least a portion of a night period. 30. The system of claim 28 wherein a new patch is used for each sleep period. 31. The system of claim 28 wherein the number of sleep periods is a fixed number of sleep periods. 32. The system of claim 28 wherein the number of sleep periods is determined by the control unit based at least in part on a confidence level of the information about the snoring event. 33. The system of claim 27 wherein the control unit includes a processor configured to generate the sleep profile utilizing an artificial intelligence algorithm. 34. The system of claim 24 wherein either or both of the sleep profile and sleep plan for the person is also based on information representative of likelihood of another person being disturbed by snoring of the person. 35. A patch comprising: a wearable component configured to allow the patch to be worn on a user; and a sensor component implemented relative to the wearable component, the sensor component including a transducer configured to generate an electrical signal based on a snoring sound. 36. The patch of claim 35 wherein the wearable component includes a patch substrate configured to allow the patch to be worn on a skin of the user. 37. The patch of claim 36 wherein the sensor component is implemented at least partially within the patch substrate. 38. The patch of claim 36 wherein the patch substrate includes an application surface configured to engage the skin of the user, and an open surface opposite from the application surface. 39. The patch of claim 35 wherein the transducer includes a microphone. 40. The patch of claim 39 wherein the microphone is implemented to receive the snoring sound incident on one of the application surface and the exposed surface. 41. The patch of claim 41 wherein the incident snoring sound includes a sound energy transmitted through air or a sound energy transmitted through a body of the user. 42. The patch of claim 40 further comprising an additional microphone implemented to receive a sound incident on the other of the application surface and the exposed surface. 43. The patch of claim 42 wherein either or both of the sound incident on the application surface and the sound incident on the exposed surface includes the snoring sound. 44. The patch of claim 35 further comprising an electronic circuit configured to process the electrical signal. 45. The patch of claim 44 wherein the electronic circuit is configured to identify a source of the snoring sound. 46. The patch of claim 45 wherein the electronic circuit is configured to perform the identification of the source of the snoring sound based on a signal processing algorithm. 47. The patch of claim 45 wherein the electronic circuit is configured to perform the identification of the source of the snoring sound based on one or more of intensity, frequency, and pattern associated with the snoring sound. 48. The patch of claim 45 wherein the user is the source of the snoring sound. 49. The patch of claim 44 wherein the electronic circuit is configured to route the electrical signal to a device external to the wearable patch. 50. The patch of claim 35 further comprising a communication component configured to communicate information associated with the electrical signal to a device external to the patch. 51. The patch of claim 50 wherein the communication component includes a transmitter configured to generate a transmit signal based on the information associated with the electrical signal. 52. The patch of claim 51 wherein the communication component further includes an antenna to support a wireless transmission of the transmit signal. 53. The patch of claim 35 further comprising an alert transducer configured to generate an alert stimulus for the user based on the electrical signal. 54. The patch of claim 53 wherein the alert stimulus generated by the alert transducer includes a vibration signal or a low-intensity sound signal. 55. The patch of claim 35 wherein the wearable component is configured to allow the patch to be secured to the user with an adhesive layer. 56. The patch of claim 35 wherein the wearable component is configured to allow the patch to be secured to a pierced body portion of the user. 57. The patch of claim 56 wherein the wearable component is configured to be worn as a body piercing device on a nose or an earlobe. 58. A method for reducing snoring activity, the method comprising: sensing, with a transducer of a patch secured to a skin of a user, a snoring sound; and generating an electrical signal based on the sensed snoring sound. 59. The method of claim 58 further comprising processing the electrical signal to identify a source of the snoring sound. 60. The method of claim 58 further comprising transmitting information associated with the electrical signal to a device external to the patch. 61. A kit for reducing snoring activity, the kit comprising: a plurality of patches implemented in a packaged format, each patch including a wearable component configured to allow the patch to be worn on a user, and a sensor component implemented relative to the wearable component, the sensor component including a transducer configured to generate an electrical signal based on a snoring sound; and a printed instruction configured to facilitate use of the patch.
